title: Midnight Token Rebounds 19% After Wanchain Bridge Hack
summary: The Midnight token (NIGHT) rebounded 19% after a hack on the Wanchain bridge briefly sent it to an all-time low. Charles Hoskinson is calling for a revamp of bridge infrastructure, advocating for zero-knowledge technology to prevent such exploits.
body:
The Midnight token (NIGHT) rebounded 19% following a significant hack on the Wanchain bridge, which initially caused the token to hit an all-time low. The exploit highlighted vulnerabilities in current bridge infrastructure, sparking a broader discussion about security in the crypto space.

Charles Hoskinson, founder of Cardano, emphasized the need for the industry to move beyond legacy bridge systems. He argued that zero-knowledge (ZK) technology could provide more secure alternatives, reducing the risk of such exploits in the future.

For everyday crypto users, this event underscores the importance of security in blockchain infrastructure. Hacks like this can lead to significant financial losses and erode trust in the ecosystem. Investors should be cautious and consider the security measures of the platforms they use.

The crypto community is now watching closely to see how Wanchain and other projects respond to this incident. The push for more secure bridge solutions could accelerate the adoption of ZK technology, potentially making the entire ecosystem more robust. Users should stay informed about developments in this area.

The exploit highlighted vulnerabilities in current bridge systems. Hoskinson argues for moving beyond legacy bridges to more secure alternatives like ZK technology.

This event underscores the importance of security in blockchain infrastructure. Users should stay informed about developments in secure bridge solutions. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-22-midnight-token-rebounds-19-after-wanchain-bridge-hack
